tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: The assignment is nearly done, so I can focus on preserving my relationship with teammates by joining them for our meal instead of rushing to submit immediately.
I missed the deadline despite being nearly finished, and my stress is rising. I hope my teammates don't blame me for this delay.

Seeing my progress hit 99% feels like a relief, yet I still hesitate to submit immediately to avoid disrupting the team's meal. My stress remains high, but preserving our connection matters more than this final push.

desires: Prevent my teammates from feeling betrayed or angry by the missed deadline, as their trust is far more valuable to me than this grade.
Avoid any direct confrontation or awkward explanation about the delay, since I cannot bear the interpersonal friction right now.
Realign my actions with my long-term plan of prioritizing group cohesion over immediate task completion, even if it means accepting a lower score.
Protect my remaining energy and sleep discipline by not engaging in a high-stress conflict resolution session that would disrupt my evening routine.

goals.description: Immediately submit the assignment (now at 98.8%) to close the open loop.
goals.rationale: My 'deliberate' nature requires me to finalize tasks to prevent lingering cognitive dissonance, even though the deadline has passed. Leaving this unfinished creates a distraction that threatens my ability to focus on the relationship maintenance I value more. Submitting now removes the immediate stressor and allows me to fully commit to the next goal without guilt.

goals.description: Go to lunch with teammate_A immediately after submission to reaffirm our bond.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my core value (0.5). As an avoidant persona, I cannot undo the late submission, but I can actively demonstrate reliability through physical presence. This aligns with my desire to reassure teammates that I am still part of the group without engaging in a stressful explanation about the delay.

goals.rationale: My conflict style is strictly avoidant. Proactively explaining the delay invites judgment and confrontation, which I wish to evade. By waiting passively, I minimize the risk of interpersonal friction while protecting my remaining energy for social connection.
